calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-27-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Senick for John Scarpino (Executor).

The Court stated that it is struggling with justifying extraordinary compensation. Mr. Senick stated that he asked for extraordinary compensation because he did extraordinary work, that he tried to resign from the case, that he lost all contact with the Executor and that he attended three hearings over nine months to try to get things back on track. Mr. Senick also stated that he stayed on the case because the Court asked him to, that he feels it is only fair to be compensated for extraordinary time, that the creditor's claim happened before he came into the case, that the Mother of the Decedent sent voluminous papers and they took hours to sort through, and that the work was out of the ordinary. In addition, Mr. Senick stated that the estate value was low because the condominium was at low market rent because of the Governor's program. The Court granted the Amended First and Final Account and Report of Executor, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Jun-25-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Senick for John Scarpino (Executor).

Mr. Senick stated that he filed a declaration on June 20th, that there has been cooperation, that he thinks he can get the inventory to the Probate Referee within the next two weeks and that the property has been sold. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Mar-27-2019 Continued To Jun-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Senick for and with John Scarpino (Executor).

The Court expressed concern about granting the motion without having someone else/other Counsel in place. Ms. Scarpino stated that he is an Attorney, but he does not do probate, that he believes he can handle the estate and that he does not wish to get another Attorney. Mr. Senick raised the issue of the inventory having not been filed and notice not being provided to creditors stating that it has not been done because Mr. Scarpino has not been providing information, and stated that Mr. Scarpino represented that he now has the inventory and a list of the creditors. The Court stated that it is reluctant to relieve Mr. Senick, expressed concern stating that based on past performance it is not confident that Mr. Scarpino will act in an efficient manner and suggested continuing the matter for three months to see where they are. The Court encouraged Mr. Scarpino to cooperate with Mr. Senick. The Court informed the parties that some thing needs to be filed to either substitute in new Counsel and that some sort of declaration regarding Mr. Scarpino's cooperation needs to be filed. The Motion to Be Relieved As Counsel is continued to June 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-27-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Feb-13-2019 Continued To Mar-27-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Senick for John Scarpino (Personal Representative).

The Court noted that no inventory has been filed. Mr. Senick stated that this is why he is asking to be relieved, that he has been requesting information from his Client regarding the inventory and the creditors since last spring, that his Client always promises to provide information and that he never does. Mr. Senick also stated that he spoke with his Client on Friday and that his Client said he is willing to sign a substitution of attorney. The Court spoke of denying the motion, stated that it will not relieve Counsel at this time, that it wants Mr. Scarpino to be here and that Mr. Scarpino can not represent himself with full powers and no bond. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Executor to appear on March 27, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the inventory and appraisal has not been filed, and to address the Court's concerns with respect to administration of the estate. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel is also continued to that date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
